next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 4

The ridge trail had been climbing since dawn, and Kem's calves were filing complaints he had no language for.

In the Vault, the longest walk was sixty meters — Conservatory to the east dining hall, always on flat stone, always in soft-soled shoes designed to make no sound. Nobody ran in the Vault. Nobody climbed. The body was a thing you maintained through inaction, like a vase, and it performed accordingly. Now his body was being asked to carry its own weight uphill through tangled forest for six consecutive hours, and it was responding with specific muscular opinions he'd never known it had.

Vel was thirty paces ahead, moving through the undergrowth with the kind of ease that came from having done this since before he was born. She didn't look back. She hadn't looked back since they'd broken camp that morning, and Kem had stopped deciding whether this was trust or indifference because both options were equally dispiriting.

The highland forest closed overhead. The trees were old — Kem didn't know what kind, couldn't tell one from another, an omission in his education he hadn't known existed until two days ago. He'd asked Vel what a particular tree was called and she'd looked at him as if he'd asked her what walking was. So he'd stopped asking about trees. The light filtered through the canopy in shifting columns, warm where it landed, cool where it didn't. The air carried the smell of earth, of something resinous and sharp, of leaves that had fallen last year and were slowly becoming soil. His pack straps had worn through the linen of his shirt and were wor...
open_hooks_to_carry: Vel's disgust at Kem's wastefulness — 286 after mere days
The terms: she guides, he follows, she controls information flow
What Vel conceals: the mechanism's specific requirement for a high-level spender
prev_tail_excerpt:
...ll sun and the sun fell on him with a direct, impersonal heat he had not prepared for, because he had not prepared for anything. His neck was already pink. His face felt tight. The air tasted different out here — drier, carrying dust and pollen, nothing like the Vault's managed atmosphere. He could feel the Ledger humming at the edge of his awareness, quieter now by twelve points. It was not a sensation he could have described to anyone who hadn't felt it — a fullness slightly less full, like a room with a few books pulled from the shelf. He barely noticed it.

He walked south. His body did what it could, which was less than he would have liked and more than he had any right to expect from a body that had spent twenty-six years doing nothing. His feet bled. His shoulders ached from holding his arms in the unfamiliar rhythm of walking. The signet ring on his finger caught the sun and flashed, and he looked at it — the Aldraste crest, the closed circle, the symbol of a House built on the principle that the best use of power was no use at all — and he kept walking, because he had started walking, and stopping would require a decision, and the only decision he had made today was to stand up, and everything after that had been momentum.

Behind him the food cooled in the pine forest. The fox ate until it couldn't move and lay on its side near the roast. The ants disassembled the cake tier by tier. The stew thickened in its bowls and the bread went stale and the Vault's gate remained open because no one inside had yet convened a committee to determine whether it should be closed.

setups_to_plant.description: Vel's careful assessment of Kem's remaining levels and her private satisfaction that he solves her problem — reads as pragmatic alliance-building, later revealed as calculated manipulation

state_deltas_required: Mutual need and mutual disgust between two people who represent opposite failures of the Ledger system
Uneasy alliance formed; Vel has knowledge, Kem has levels, neither trusts the other
